2606.26744.title: HyperDFlash: MHC-Aligned Block Speculative Decoding with Gated Residual Reduction
2606.26744.paper_summary: We present HyperDFlash, a block-parallel speculative decoding framework tailored to the novel multi-hyper-connection (MHC) architecture proposed by DeepSeek-V4. Despite the strong initial-token drafting performance of the native Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) module in DeepSeek-V4, its draft accuracy degrades sharply at later positions, as error accumulation from unverified intermediate tokens harms acceptance rates. Although the original DFlash method supports efficient one-pass block drafting, it cannot be seamlessly adapted to the MHC paradigm, since the multi-path residual stream of DeepSeek-V4 induces feature misalignment with conventional drafting designs. To resolve this mismatch, we propose two model-aligned optimizations for MHC residual streams. First, we adopt pre-collapse residual states as the exclusive conditioning signal, preserving multi-path structural information and aligning the drafter with the native prediction pathway of the target model. Second, we replace the heavy generic linear compressor with a lightweight gated residual reducer, whose parameters are inherited from the built-in hyper-connection head. This design yields input-aware path aggregation with three orders of magnitude fewer parameters while maintaining architectural alignment. We further enhance training via a targeted KL distillation loss applied to the LM-head, which regularizes predictions against the full target probability distribution and improves draft quality at early training stages. Experiments across math reasoning, code synthesis, and conversational benchmarks show that HyperDFlash consistently outperforms both the native MTP baseline and vanilla DFlash adaptation. It achieves substantial gains in average accepted draft length and decoding speedup, validating the effectiveness of MHC alignment, gated reduction, and targeted distillation for high-performance speculative decoding.
2606.26744.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 HyperDFlash，一個針對 DeepSeek-V4 提出的新型多超連接 (MHC) 架構量身打造的區塊並行推測解碼框架。儘管 DeepSeek-V4 中原生的多標記預測 (MTP) 模組在初始標記草擬性能上表現強勁，但其草擬準確性在後續位置急劇下降，因為未經驗證的中間標記所造成的錯誤累積損害了接受率。雖然原始的 DFlash 方法支持高效的一次性區塊草擬，但由於 DeepSeek-V4 的多路徑殘差流導致與傳統草擬設計的特徵不對齊，因此無法無縫適應 MHC 範式。為了解決這一不匹配，我們為 MHC 殘差流提出了兩種模型對齊的優化方案。首先，我們採用預崩潰的殘差狀態作為專屬的條件信號，保留多路徑結構信息，並使草擬者與目標模型的原生預測路徑對齊。其次，我們用輕量級的門控殘差縮減器替換了重型的通用線性壓縮器，其參數繼承自內建的超連接頭。這一設計實現了輸入感知的路徑聚合，參數數量減少了三個量級，同時保持了架構對齊。我們進一步通過針對 LM-head 應用的目標 KL 蒸餾損失來增強訓練，這一損失對預測進行正則化，使其對應全目標概率分佈，並提高早期訓練階段的草擬質量。在數學推理、代碼合成和對話基準測試中的實驗顯示，HyperDFlash 始終優於原生 MTP 基線和普通 DFlash 適應。它在平均接受草擬長度和解碼加速方面取得了顯著增益，驗證了 MHC 對齊、門控縮減和針對性蒸餾在高性能推測解碼中的有效性。
2606.26744.author: Luxi Lin et.al.
2606.26744.authors: Luxi Lin, Shuang Peng, Rui Ma, Junhao Hua, Shuwei Fan, Zhengda Qin, Qiang Wang, Hongjian Sun, Fangmin Chen, Songwei Liu
